title: What to know about Astragalus membranaceus before using it medicinally
intro_desc: Astragalus membranaceus, commonly known as huang qi, is a plant used in traditional Chinese medicine. It is primarily used to treat kidney and lung disorders, as well as to boost the immune system in cases of chronic fatigue and autoimmune diseases. The herb has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its main health benefits, including cardiovascular health and cancer prevention. Its therapeutic properties include immunomodulatory, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities, which are attributed to bioactive compounds such as flavonoids, saponins, and polysaccharides. Astragalus is often prepared as a decoction, tea, or capsule, and is commonly used to treat conditions such as bronchitis and chronic bronchitis.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of ethnopharmacology, the rhizomes of Zingiber officinale have been found to have several health benefits, including the reduction of inflammation and fever in rats, as well as the inhibition of the growth of both Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria. Additionally, the study found that the extract of Zingiber officinale has analgesic properties, although it was ineffective in suppressing writhing induced by intraperitoneal acetic acid. Furthermore, the study demonstrated that Zingiber officinale has hypoglycaemic effects, with the extract producing blood glucose lowering in rabbits.
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Astragalus membranaceus are alleviating fatigue and anemia through its rich content of isoflavones and saponins, which stimulate erythropoiesis and improve iron metabolism. Astragalus membranaceus is also used to relieve cough and bronchitis due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids that modulate the immune system and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract. Additionally, this plant has been employed to treat chronic fatigue syndrome, diabetes, and digestive issues such as diarrhea, thanks to its immunomodulatory and antioxidant effects, which are largely attributed to the polysaccharides and triterpenoids present in the plant. Its immunomodulatory and antiviral properties, specifically due to the presence of astragaloside IV, make it a valuable treatment for respiratory infections and as an adjunctive therapy for cancer treatment. Astragalus membranaceus also exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its potential therapeutic benefits in various health conditions.

uses_list.description: This herb is used for fatigue due to its adaptogenic properties, which help the body to cope with stress and promote balance in the body's physiological processes.
Its expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and relieve coughs and congestion associated with respiratory infections.
The herb's ability to enhance immune function, reduce oxidative stress, and improve cellular energy production may help alleviate symptoms of chronic fatigue syndrome.
It is used to treat anemia due to its iron content and ability to stimulate red blood cell production, which can help to improve oxygen delivery to tissues and alleviate anemia symptoms.
Research suggests that this herb may help to regulate blood sugar levels and improve insulin sensitivity, making it a potential natural treatment for diabetes.
Its antimicrobial properties help to combat bacterial and viral infections, such as the common cold and flu, by inhibiting the growth of pathogens and reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract.

The herb is used to treat digestive issues, such as constipation and diarrhea, due to its ability to regulate bowel function, improve gut motility, and reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract.

Its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties help to relieve bronchitis symptoms, such as coughing and shortness of breath, by loosening and clearing mucus from the lungs and reducing inflammation in the airways.

The herb is used to treat diarrhea due to its ability to regulate bowel function, reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, and improve gut health, which can help to alleviate diarrhea symptoms and promote normal bowel function.

Research suggests that this herb may have anti-tumor and anti-cancer properties, which can help to inhibit cancer cell growth, induce apoptosis, and reduce the risk of cancer recurrence, making it a potential natural adjunct to cancer treatment.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Astragalus membranaceus is used to treat chronic fatigue syndrome by enhancing immune function. It is also used in cancer treatment protocols to reduce side effects and boost the immune system's response to chemotherapy. Astragalus membranaceus has been shown to be effective in managing autoimmune diseases, such as rheumatoid arthritis, by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response. Additionally, it is used to support HIV/AIDS patients by stimulating the immune system and improving quality of life. Astragalus membranaceus has been found to be effective in treating a range of conditions including respiratory infections, cardiovascular disease, immune system support, immune deficiency, diabetes, and inflammation, by modulating the immune response and reducing oxidative stress.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional chinese medicine, Astragalus membranaceus is used to treat fatigue and cough, both of which are alleviated by its immunomodulatory properties. It is also employed to address anemia, with its ability to increase hemoglobin production and red blood cell count. Additionally, Astragalus membranaceus has been used to treat diarrhea, a condition for which it has been shown to improve intestinal integrity. Furthermore, it is used to manage diabetes, as it has been found to reduce blood glucose levels and improve insulin sensitivity. The plant is also used to treat a range of respiratory conditions, including bronchitis, respiratory infections, and asthma, as well as digestive issues and inflammation, all of which are thought to be alleviated by its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Astragalus membranaceus is used to treat fatigue by increasing energy levels and improving vitality. Astragalus membranaceus is also used to alleviate cough and respiratory infections, such as bronchitis, by reducing inflammation and mucus production. Additionally, it is used to address digestive issues, including diarrhea, by promoting gut health and regulating bowel movements. Furthermore, Astragalus membranaceus is used to support individuals with anemia by increasing iron absorption and improving red blood cell production. The plant is also used to treat diabetes by regulating blood sugar levels and improving insulin sensitivity, while also offering immune system support by stimulating the production of white blood cells and activating immune cells.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Astragalus membranaceus is used to treat various health conditions, with its adaptogenic properties making it effective in alleviating fatigue. Astragalus is also known to help manage diabetes by regulating blood sugar levels and improving insulin sensitivity. Furthermore, the plant's expectorant properties make it useful in treating cough, particularly in cases of bronchitis, where it helps to loosen and clear mucus from the airways. Additionally, Astragalus membranaceus is used to combat anemia by increasing red blood cell count and hemoglobin levels, while its immunomodulatory effects help in combating respiratory infections. This plant is also beneficial in treating asthma, diarrhea, digestive issues, and hypertension, making it a valuable component in unani medicine.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Astragalus membranaceus is used to treat a variety of conditions, including fatigue, which it has been shown to alleviate by increasing red blood cell count and hemoglobin levels. It is also used to address anemia, as it has been found to stimulate erythropoiesis and improve iron metabolism. Furthermore, Astragalus membranaceus has been used to support patients with cancer, particularly those undergoing chemotherapy, by enhancing their immune response and reducing the side effects of treatment. In addition to these applications, it is also used to help manage diabetes, as it has been shown to improve insulin sensitivity and glucose metabolism. Additionally, Astragalus membranaceus is used to treat digestive issues, weakened immune system, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, respiratory issues, and cough, all of which are thought to be alleviated by its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
benefits_description: Astragalus membranaceus reduces inflammation by suppressing pro-inflammatory cytokines and NF-κB signaling pathways, while it boosts immune system by activating macrophages and natural killer cells, and improves digestion through the regulation of gut microbiota and enhancement of gut motility. Astragalus membranaceus lowers blood pressure by reducing sympathetic nervous activity and increasing parasympathetic activity, which fights cancer cells by inducing apoptosis and inhibiting proliferation of cancer cells, and enhances anti-oxidation by scavenging free radicals and increasing glutathione levels. Astragalus membranaceus reduces stress levels by modulating hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, supports liver function by improving liver cell viability and reducing liver enzyme levels, and increases energy levels by increasing ATP production and improving mitochondrial function, and prevents anemia by stimulating erythropoiesis and reducing oxidative damage to erythrocytes.

benefits_list.description: This plant has anti-inflammatory properties due to the presence of flavonoids, saponins, and polysaccharides, which can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases.

The plant contains polysaccharides and other compounds that can stimulate the production of white blood cells, enhance the activity of immune cells, and increase the levels of antibodies, thus boosting the immune system and helping the body to fight off infections.

The plant has prebiotic properties, which can help to promote the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, improve digestion, and enhance the absorption of nutrients, thus alleviating symptoms associated with digestive disorders such as irritable bowel syndrome and diarrhea.

The plant contains compounds that can help to relax blood vessels, improve blood flow, and reduce blood pressure, thus alleviating symptoms associated with hypertension and reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease.

The plant has been shown to have anti-tumor and anti-cancer properties due to the presence of flavonoids, saponins, and other compounds that can inhibit the growth of cancer cells, induce apoptosis, and reduce the formation of new blood vessels, thus helping to prevent the spread of cancer.

The plant contains antioxidants such as flavonoids and saponins that can help to neutralize free radicals, reduce oxidative stress, and protect cells from damage, thus alleviating symptoms associated with aging and age-related diseases.

The plant has been shown to have adaptogenic properties, which can help to reduce stress levels, improve mood, and enhance cognitive function by regulating the body's response to stress and promoting relaxation.

The plant contains compounds that can help to protect the liver from damage, improve liver function, and enhance the removal of toxins, thus alleviating symptoms associated with liver disease and reducing the risk of liver damage.

The plant has been shown to have adaptogenic properties, which can help to increase energy levels, improve physical performance, and enhance endurance by regulating the body's response to stress and promoting relaxation.
The plant contains iron and other minerals that are essential for the production of red blood cells, thus helping to prevent anemia and alleviate symptoms associated with this condition.

benefits_circulatory_system_description: Astragalus membranaceus improves blood flow by increasing nitric oxide production, a potent vasodilator, which relaxes blood vessels and allows for more efficient circulation. This plant reduces blood pressure through its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and reactive oxygen species that contribute to hypertension. Astragalus membranaceus also enhances circulation by stimulating the production of ATP and improving endothelial function, allowing for more efficient oxygen delivery to tissues. Regular consumption of Astragalus membranaceus has been shown to regulate heart rate by modulating the autonomic nervous system and reducing sympathetic tone, resulting in a more stable and efficient cardiovascular system. Additionally, Astragalus membranaceus supports vascular health by increasing the production of collagen and elastin, protecting against the degeneration of blood vessels, and reducing inflammation levels, while also protecting cardiac cells from damage and improving cardiovascular function by increasing red blood cells and boosting oxygen delivery.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Astragalus membranaceus relaxes digestive tract through its anti-inflammatory properties, reducing spasms and tension. This plant stimulates gut lining, promoting a healthy environment for beneficial bacteria to thrive, which in turn reduces inflammation response. By soothing irritated mucous, Astragalus membranaceus helps to prevent the buildup of toxins and waste in the digestive system, while also boosting immune system function through its immunomodulatory effects. Astragalus membranaceus also prevents gut damage by protecting the epithelial lining and increasing digestive enzymes, which aids in the breakdown and absorption of nutrients. Additionally, the antioxidant and antimicrobial properties of Astragalus membranaceus enhance gut permeability, supporting a balanced gut microbiome and overall digestive health, and protect gut barrier from pathogens and toxins.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Astragalus membranaceus regulates cortisol levels by modulating the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, thereby mitigating the negative effects of chronic stress on the body. Astragalus membranaceus suppresses adrenal fatigue by stimulating the production of cortisol and other hormones essential for stress adaptation. It balances thyroid hormones by modulating the expression of genes involved in thyroid hormone regulation and transport. Astragalus membranaceus stabilizes insulin sensitivity by inhibiting the activity of protein tyrosine phosphatase 1B, a key regulator of insulin signaling. Astragalus membranaceus activates adrenal recovery by promoting the expression of genes involved in adrenal regeneration, supports pituitary health by modulating the production of growth hormone and other pituitary hormones, boosts metabolic rate by increasing the expression of genes involved in energy metabolism, reduces inflammation response by inhibiting the activity of pro-inflammatory cytokines, and enhances pancreatic function and normalizes ovarian function by modulating the expression of genes involved in glucose and hormone regulation.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Astragalus membranaceus protects skin from environmental stressors and UV damage due to its antioxidant properties, which neutralize free radicals and prevent oxidative stress. It also repairs damage to skin cells and tissue by stimulating collagen production, a process facilitated by its ability to promote collagen synthesis. Additionally, Astragalus membranaceus boosts immunity by activating macrophages and natural killer cells, which enhances its ability to fight infections. This plant enhances healing by promoting angiogenesis, the formation of new blood vessels, and reduces inflammation by inhibiting pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes. Astragalus membranaceus soothes irritation by modulating the immune response and promoting regeneration by stimulating the production of growth factors and cellular renewal, which ultimately stimulates renewal of skin cells and tissue.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Astragalus membranaceus boosts lymph flow due to its diuretic properties, which stimulate the kidneys to produce more urine, thereby increasing lymph circulation. Stimulating lymph nodes is another effect of Astragalus membranaceus, attributed to its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce the swelling and inflammation of lymph nodes. Astragalus membranaceus enhances lymph function through its antioxidant activity, neutralizing free radicals that can damage lymphatic tissues and impair lymph function. The anti-edematous properties of Astragalus membranaceus reduce lymph swelling, while its vasodilatory effects increase lymph drainage, allowing the body to remove waste and toxins more efficiently. By supporting lymph health and increasing white blood cell production due to its immunomodulatory properties, Astragalus membranaceus also improves the immune response and reduces lymph inflammation.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Astragalus membranaceus reduces inflammation through its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are essential in alleviating joint pain and discomfort associated with conditions such as arthritis. Improved joint mobility in individuals suffering from osteoarthritis has been observed due to the plant's ability to promote the repair and regeneration of cartilage tissue. Astragalus membranaceus enhances muscle strength by increasing the production of creatine kinase, an enzyme that plays a crucial role in energy production and muscle contraction. Its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties also accelerate wound healing by reducing the risk of infection and promoting a conducive environment for tissue repair. Soothing muscle pain and relaxing muscle tension in individuals with fibromyalgia have been attributed to the plant's ability to decrease the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and promote the release of pain-relieving neurotransmitters.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Astragalus membranaceus boosts nerve function by enhancing neurotransmitter release, which is attributed to its neurotrophic properties. This plant reduces nerve damage through its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, protecting neurons from oxidative stress and inflammation. Astragalus membranaceus calms the nervous system by modulating the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, reducing cortisol levels and promoting relaxation. Its neuroprotective and anti-apoptotic properties improve nerve regeneration by preventing neuronal cell death and promoting the growth of new neurons, leading to enhanced brain function and improved cognitive function. Astragalus membranaceus also supports neuroprotection by reducing anxiety levels through its anxiolytic effects, improves sleep quality by modulating the body's circadian rhythms, protects nerve cells through its anti- excitotoxicity properties, and enhances cognitive function by improving memory and learning capabilities.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Astragalus membranaceus supports ovulation by stimulating the pituitary gland to release gonadotropin-releasing hormone, which in turn regulates the reproductive system's hormonal balance. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties regulate menstrual cycles by reducing uterine contractions and preventing endometrial damage. Astragalus membranaceus stimulates fertility by increasing the production of follicle-stimulating hormone, which promotes ovulation and egg maturation. Its antioxidant properties improve sperm quality by protecting against oxidative stress and DNA damage, ultimately reducing infertility. Astragalus membranaceus also enhances hormone balance, promotes egg development, increases testosterone levels, boosts libido, and protects ovaries through its adaptogenic and anti-stress properties.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Astragalus membranaceus reduces inflammation by possessing anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate respiratory issues. This plant relaxes airway spasms with its antispasmodic properties, making it easier to breathe. Astragalus membranaceus also boosts immunity by containing immunomodulatory compounds that stimulate the immune system, protecting against infections. Additionally, it cools down fever through its antipyretic properties, alleviating discomfort and reducing the risk of complications. By clearing mucus blockage with its expectorant properties and soothing irritated tissues with its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, Astragalus membranaceus prevents respiratory infections, eases breathing difficulty, protects lung damage, and activates antioxidants to reduce oxidative stress.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Astragalus membranaceus protects bladder by exhibiting antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of pathogens that can cause infections. Preventing infections is also achieved through its immunomodulatory effects, which enhance the body's ability to fight off pathogens. Curing cystitis is possible due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce the swelling and pain associated with this condition. The plant reduces inflammation in the urinary tract, healing ulcers that can develop as a result of chronic inflammation. The antioxidant properties of Astragalus membranaceus soothe irritation in the urinary tract, preventing kidney damage and treating nephritis, while also treating prostatitis and reducing oxidation to prevent further damage.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Astragalus membranaceus are numerous, and it has been found to possess antimicrobial activity, inhibiting the growth of various pathogenic microorganisms. This plant has also been identified as a rich source of antioxidants, which can scavenge free radicals and mitigate oxidative stress, thereby exhibiting anti-inflammatory effects and modulating the immune system. Additionally, Astragalus membranaceus has been reported to possess antiviral properties, which can inhibit the replication of certain viruses, and it has also been found to be cardioprotective, mitigating cardiovascular disorders by reducing lipid peroxidation and improving blood lipid profiles. Furthermore, the bioactive compounds present in this plant, including flavonoids and saponins, have been found to exhibit neuroprotective effects, potentially mitigating neurodegenerative diseases such as Alzheimer's and Parkinson's. Astragalus membranaceus has also been traditionally used to alleviate pain and has been found to possess antibacterial properties, in addition to its previously mentioned properties.

properties_list.description: Astragalus membranaceus has been found to exhibit antimicrobial activity against various microorganisms, including bacteria and fungi, due to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can inhibit the growth of these microorganisms.

Astragalus membranaceus contains antioxidants such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can scavenge free radicals and protect cells from oxidative damage, potentially reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease.

Astragalus membranaceus has anti-inflammatory properties, which may be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and saponins, that can inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, thereby reducing inflammation and potentially alleviating conditions such as arthritis.

Astragalus membranaceus has been found to possess immunomodulatory properties, which may be attributed to the presence of polysaccharides and other bioactive compounds, that can stimulate the immune system and enhance its response to infections, potentially increasing resistance to diseases such as tuberculosis.

Astragalus membranaceus has been found to exhibit antiviral activity against certain viruses, including HIV and herpes simplex virus, due to the presence of flavonoids and polysaccharides, which can inhibit viral replication and potentially alleviate viral infections.

Astragalus membranaceus has been found to possess cardioprotective properties, which may be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, that can protect the cardiovascular system from damage, potentially reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease and improving cardiac function.

Astragalus membranaceus has been found to exhibit neuroprotective properties, which may be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, that can protect neurons from damage, potentially alleviating conditions such as Alzheimer's disease and Parkinson's disease.

Astragalus membranaceus contains antioxidants such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can scavenge free radicals and protect cells from oxidative damage, potentially reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease.

Astragalus membranaceus has been found to possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, that can inhibit the production of pain-causing chemicals and reduce inflammation, potentially alleviating pain associated with conditions such as arthritis.

Astragalus membranaceus has been found to exhibit antibacterial activity against various bacteria, including Staphylococcus aureus and Escherichia coli, due to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can inhibit the growth of these microorganisms.

constituents.description: Saponins are amphipathic glycosides with foaming properties and hemolytic activity.
Astragalus membranaceus flavonoids are known for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory properties.
Phenolic acids are water-soluble compounds that exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and free radical scavenging properties.
Polysaccharides from Astragalus membranaceus have immunomodulatory, anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Astragalosides are a group of saponin glycosides that exhibit anti-inflammatory, immunomodulatory, antioxidant, and cardiovascular protective properties.
Coumarins are a class of compounds with anticoagulant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.
Astragalus membranaceus Alkaloids have demonstrated anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties, as well as potential anti-cancer effects.

Astragalus membranaceus contains glycosides, which are complex molecules composed of sugar units attached to a non-sugar core, with properties including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory activities.
Triterpenoids are a class of bioactive compounds found in Astragalus membranaceus, possessing anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties.
A type of Terpenoids found in Astragalus membranaceus have been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

constituents_desc: The main medicinal constituents of Astragalus membranaceus are complex and multifaceted, primarily consisting of saponins, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects. Additionally, flavonoids such as kaempferol and quercetin have been isolated from Astragalus membranaceus, demonstrating antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Phenolic acids, including ferulic acid and sinapic acid, have also been identified in the plant, contributing to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Polysaccharides, including astragalosides, have been isolated from Astragalus membranaceus and have been shown to have immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory effects, as well as promoting wound healing and anti-aging. Astragalosides, in particular, have been demonstrated to have cardiotonic and diuretic effects, as well as exhibiting anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties.
parts_desc: The most used parts of Astragalus membranaceus for medicinal purposes are roots and rhizomes. The roots primarily contain saponins, flavonoids, and polyphenols, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, while the rhizomes contain volatile oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The roots' bioactive compounds, particularly astragalosides, are responsible for their analgesic and antipyretic effects, making them useful in treating fever and pain. In contrast, the rhizomes' bioactive compounds, such as volatile oils and flavonoids, contribute to their expectorant and anti-asthmatic properties, making them beneficial for respiratory issues. The medicinal properties of both roots and rhizomes are attributed to their ability to modulate the immune system and exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.

parts_list.description: The roots of this plant contain major bioactive compounds such as astragaloside IV, astragaloside I, and isoflavones, which exhibit immunomodulatory, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, contributing to its use in traditional medicine for promoting cardiovascular health, enhancing immune function, and reducing oxidative stress, thereby offering potential benefits in preventing chronic diseases such as atherosclerosis, heart failure, and cancer.

The rhizomes of this plant are rich in bioactive compounds including flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects, making them useful in traditional medicine for treating various conditions such as bronchitis, cough, and fever, as well as for its potential benefits in reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and modulating immune responses, thereby offering protective effects against oxidative stress, inflammation, and immune-related disorders.

preparations.description: Tincture made from this plant is used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health, and is traditionally used to treat chronic fatigue syndrome, upper respiratory infections, and hepatitis.
preparations.overview: Astragalus membranaceus tincture, a concentrated liquid extract of the roots and rhizomes of the Astragalus membranaceus plant, is commonly used to treat fatigue, respiratory infections, weakened immune system, diabetes, cancer, hypertension, inflammation, cardiovascular disease, digestive issues and chronic bronchitis. It is a frequently used herbal remedy, with Astragalus membranaceus tincture being a common preparation among herbalists and practitioners. The tincture is known to have a stimulating effect on the immune system, increasing the production of white blood cells. The preparation is made by infusing the roots and rhizomes of the plant in a solvent, typically ethanol or glycerin, which requires moderate difficulty, as it demands precise measurements and controlled conditions to ensure the quality and potency of the final product.
recipe: Gather 1 part of Astragalus membranaceus roots and rhizomes and dry them completely.
Combine the dried roots and rhizomes with 3 parts of 80% ethanol in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the container occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store them in a cool, dark place; the recommended dosage is 20-30 drops, 2-3 times a day.

preparations.description: Capsule made from this plant is used to treat fatigue, infection, and inflammation by modulating the immune system and reducing oxidative stress, and is commonly used to prevent respiratory and gastrointestinal infections, as well as to improve the immune function in patients undergoing chemotherapy or surgery.
preparations.overview: Astragalus membranaceus capsule is a traditional herbal remedy used to treat various health conditions, including fatigue, diabetes, hypertension, weakened immune system, anemia, cough, inflammation, bronchitis, and autoimmune disorders. It's one of the most commonly used preparations in traditional Chinese medicine, indicating its widespread efficacy and reliability. Its therapeutic effects are attributed to its ability to stimulate the immune system, improve insulin sensitivity, and reduce inflammation. The capsule is typically made from the roots and rhizomes of the Astragalus membranaceus plant, which are rich in bioactive compounds. The preparation process is relatively straightforward, involving drying, cutting, and encapsulating the plant material, making it widely accessible and affordable.
recipe: Gather and clean 100g of Astragalus membranaceus roots and rhizomes.
Dry the roots and rhizomes in a low-temperature oven (50°C) for 2 hours.
Grind the dried roots and rhizomes into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powder with 100g of starch and 10g of gelatin to create a uniform blend.
Fill 100 herbal capsules with the blend, with a recommended dosage of 2 capsules, 2-3 times a day.

preparations.description: Tea made from this plant is traditionally used to boost the immune system, improve fatigue, and treat various infections, including respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
preparations.overview: Astragalus membranaceus tea is a traditional herbal remedy used to treat fatigue, weakened immune system, hypertension, diabetes, anemia, cough, inflammation, digestive issues, bronchitis, and common cold. It's one of the most commonly consumed herbal teas worldwide due to its perceived health benefits. The preparation has a diuretic effect, which is its most common effect. The roots, stems, and flowers of Astragalus membranaceus are commonly used to make this herbal tea, and the preparation process is relatively simple, requiring only hot water to extract the active compounds.
recipe: Gather 1-2 teaspoons of dried Astragalus membranaceus roots for every 8 oz of water.
Place the dried roots in a teapot or infuser.
Pour 8 oz of boiling water over the roots and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids, then add a few dried flowers of Astragalus membranaceus for garnish.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea per day, taking a 1-2 week break every 2-3 months for maintenance.

preparations.description: Decoction made from this plant is used to treat chronic fatigue syndrome, improve immune function, and reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in patients with chronic diseases.
preparations.overview: Astragalus membranaceus decoction is used to treat fatigue, cough, diabetes, hypertension, anemia, weakened immune system, bronchitis, diarrhea, and cancer. It is a common herbal preparation, widely used in traditional medicine. Its effects include reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and enhanced antioxidant activity. The decoction is made from the roots and rhizomes of Astragalus membranaceus, a plant native to China and Mongolia. Preparation of the decoction is moderately difficult, requiring proper selection of plant material, measurement of ingredients, and controlled boiling time to achieve optimal extraction of active compounds.
recipe: Gather 10-20 grams of Astragalus membranaceus roots and rhizomes.
Rinse the roots and rhizomes with clean water, and then chop them into smaller pieces.
Combine the chopped roots and rhizomes with 1 liter of water in a saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer for 30-40 minutes.
Strain the decoction and drink 20-30 mL, 2-3 times a day, as needed.

preparations.description: Salve made from this plant is applied topically to treat wounds, skin lesions, and infections, and to reduce inflammation and swelling.
preparations.overview: Astragalus membranaceus salve is used to treat burns, wound infection, inflammation, wound healing, skin irritation, bruises, eczema, cough, chronic fatigue, and bronchitis. It is an uncommonly used preparation, often overlooked in favor of more mainstream treatments. However, its unique effects have been observed in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is valued for its adaptogenic properties. The salve is typically made from the roots and rhizomes of the Astragalus membranaceus plant, which requires careful harvesting and processing to extract its medicinal compounds. Due to the complex and time-consuming nature of this process, Astragalus membranaceus salve is considered difficult to make and often requires the expertise of an experienced herbalist.
recipe: Gather 1 cup of dried Astragalus membranaceus roots and rhizomes, and 1/2 cup of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Combine the dried roots and rhizomes with the carrier oil in a double boiler, and heat the mixture over low heat for 2-3 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
Add 10-15 drops of vitamin E oil and 5-10 drops of lavender essential oil to the liquid mixture, and stir well.
Pour the mixture into small glass containers, and label them with the dosage instructions: apply topically 2-3 times a day, as needed, in a thin layer no larger than 1 inch in diameter.

preparations.description: Powder made from this plant is used to support immune function, reduce inflammation and fatigue, and treat chronic infections and autoimmune disorders.
preparations.overview: Astragalus membranaceus powder is a herbal preparation used to treat various health conditions, including fatigue, diabetes, hypertension, chronic cough, weakened immune system, anemia, inflammation, bronchitis, cancer, and influenza. It's a commonly used preparation in traditional Chinese medicine, making it a well-known remedy among practitioners. The effect of Astragalus membranaceus powder is its ability to stimulate the immune system and increase its ability to fight off infections. This preparation is typically made from the roots, stems, and flowers of the Astragalus membranaceus plant, which are harvested, dried, and then processed into a powder. The process of making Astragalus membranaceus powder is moderately complex, requiring careful consideration of the plant's quality and the processing methods to ensure the final product's potency and effectiveness.
recipe: Gather 1 kg of dried Astragalus membranaceus roots and 200g of dried Astragalus membranaceus flowers.
Dry the roots and flowers further in a low-temperature oven (50°C) for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Grind the roots and flowers into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder or mortar and pestle.
Sift the powder through a 100-mesh sieve to remove any lumps or large particles.
Store the Astragalus membranaceus herbal powder in an airtight container and consume 1-2 grams per day as recommended by a healthcare professional.

preparation_desc: The most used herbal preparation of Astragalus membranaceus for medicinal purposes are tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and pneumonia, due to their immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory properties. Astragalus membranaceus is also available in capsule form, particularly for its adaptogenic effects, supporting the body's natural response to stress and fatigue. Tea infusions of the plant are traditionally used to boost the immune system and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, while decoctions are used for their demulcent properties, soothing irritated mucous membranes. Salves prepared from Astragalus membranaceus are applied topically to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and dermatitis, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Astragalus membranaceus powder is commonly used as a dietary supplement, often in combination with other herbs, to enhance its immunomodulatory and antioxidant properties.

side_effects.side_effect_description: White blood cell count decreases in patients taking Astragalus membranaceus due to immunosuppressive effect.
Astragalus membranaceus interferes with immunosuppressive medications.
Astragalus membranaceus may exacerbate autoimmune diseases such as rheumatoid arthritis and lupus.
Astragalus membranaceus can exacerbate autoimmune disorders such as rheumatoid arthritis in susceptible individuals.
Astragalus membranaceus may interact with warfarin, increasing anticoagulant effect
Astragalus membranaceus increases the sensitivity of the immune system to antigens.
Increased sensitivity to allergens or medications

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Astragalus membranaceus are an increase in blood pressure due to its diuretic properties, a decrease in potassium levels caused by excessive diuresis. Improper use of the plant can induce arrhythmias in individuals with pre-existing heart conditions, particularly those taking digitalis, a medication also used to treat arrhythmias. Astragalus membranaceus may aggravate symptoms of diabetes due to its potential to lower blood sugar levels and interact with diabetes medications. In some cases, improper use of the plant can exacerbate kidney problems, such as nephrotic syndrome, by further impairing kidney function. The plant's immunomodulatory effects may suppress the immune system, amplifying the risk of infections, and hypersensitize individuals to other allergens or medications.

precautions.precaution_description: Use under medical supervision due to potential interactions with blood thinners, diabetes medications, and immunosuppressants.
Monitor for hypersensitivity reactions such as skin redness or itching if Astragalus membranaceus is ingested or applied topically.
Astragalus membranaceus may lower blood sugar in people with diabetes; monitor blood sugar levels frequently.

Astragalus membranaceus may decrease fertility in both men and women due to its estrogenic activity and potential effect on reproductive hormones, therefore its use during pregnancy is contraindicated.
Astragalus membranaceus may inhibit milk production, affecting infant nutrition.
Astragalus membranaceus may lower blood sugar levels in people with diabetes.

precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Astragalus membranaceus medicinally are consulting a doctor, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions, to ensure safe use. Check for allergies, as this plant may cause skin irritation or allergic reactions in some individuals. Monitor blood sugar levels closely, as Astragalus membranaceus may lower blood sugar levels and interact with diabetes medications. Avoid using Astragalus membranaceus during pregnancy, as it may stimulate the uterus and cause preterm labor. Be cautious with diabetes, as the plant's blood sugar-lowering effects may exacerbate hypoglycemia in diabetic patients, particularly when combined with other medications.
identification: Astragalus membranaceus is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Fabaceae, native to China, Mongolia, and Russia. It grows up to 1.5 meters in height with a woody base, branching stems, and pinnate leaves composed of 5-9 pairs of leaflets. The leaves are elliptical, 3-9 centimeters long, and 1-3 centimeters wide, with a membranous leaflet base. The plant produces white or purplish flowers in axillary racemes, 2-4 centimeters long, in July and August. Astragalus membranaceus is widely distributed in the mountainous regions of northern China, specifically in the provinces of Gansu, Qinghai, and Xinjiang, as well as in the mountains of Mongolia and southern Siberia.
cultivation: Astragalus membranaceus is a perennial herb native to China, exhibiting a growth habit of spreading stems and rhizomes, allowing for efficient propagation and cultivation. It thrives in well-drained, slightly alkaline soils, and full sun to partial shade, requiring temperatures between 15-25°C for optimal growth. Watering should be moderate, with adequate moisture during the first year and reduced watering in subsequent years to prevent root rot. To promote healthy growth, it is essential to maintain a balanced nitrogen-phosphorus-potassium fertilizer ratio, and avoid over-fertilizing, which can lead to reduced yields. Regular pruning and division can also enhance the plant's adaptability and increase its medicinal properties.
history: Astragalus membranaceus is a traditional Chinese medicinal herb known for its immunomodulatory and adaptogenic properties. It has been used for over 2,000 years to treat various conditions, including fever, fatigue, and respiratory infections. In traditional Chinese medicine, Astragalus membranaceus is classified as a "qi-tonifying" herb, suggesting its potential to enhance energy and vitality. Modern studies have confirmed its ability to stimulate the immune system, increase white blood cell count, and exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Astragalus membranaceus is also being researched for its potential to prevent and treat diseases such as cancer, diabetes, and cardiovascular disorders.
